name: United Kingdom (White Ensign)

colors.symbolism: the blue of the Union canton

description: A white field bearing the red Cross of St George extending to the edges, with the Union Flag in the canton.
history: The White Ensign became the sole ensign of the Royal Navy in 1864, when the colour-coded squadron system (red, white and blue squadrons) was abolished. It is also flown by the Royal Yacht Squadron and at Royal Navy shore establishments.

features.symbolism: a red Greek cross extending to the edges of the white field
the Union Flag, marking the ensign as British
